big_picture: Prime Minister Modi will chair a meeting with Chief Ministers tomorrow to discuss the West Asia crisis. The meeting comes as the crisis escalates, with India facing risks to its energy supplies and economic growth. Meanwhile, the Union Government has introduced the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026 in Lok Sabha, which aims to prevent misuse of foreign contributions. In a related development, India has seen a spike in social media censorship amid the Iran war, with activists sounding the alarm over the removal of content criticizing the government. The Supreme Court-appointed panel has urged the Centre to withdraw the 2026 trans rights amendment bill, citing concerns over its provisions.

story_clusters.headline: PM Modi to Chair Meeting With CMs Tomorrow on West Asia Crisis
story_clusters.summary: Prime Minister Modi will chair a meeting with Chief Ministers tomorrow to discuss coordinated action on the West Asia Crisis. The crisis, fueled by the US-Iran war, has raised concerns over oil and inflation risks, impacting India's economy. The conflict has also disrupted energy supplies, prompting India to ask the auto industry to optimize production.

Foreign.summary:
The Union Government in India has notified the provisions of the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, aiming to bring sports governance under a unified framework. 

Iran has allowed passage through the Strait of Hormuz for certain countries, including China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan, with India being the fifth country on the list. 

The Delhi High Court has quashed a proclamation order and a Look-Out Circular issued against an Australian citizen, stating that no effort was made to serve the accused abroad.

Legal.summary:
The Union Government notified the provisions of the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, which aims to regulate sports governance in India. 

The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) in Delhi refused to consider an application for advance ruling on the classification of roasted arecanuts, citing that the issue was already decided by a High Court judgment. 

The Allahabad High Court's 1971 judgment did not declare the Ramayana a fictional story, contrary to claims circulating on social media.

Governance.summary:
The Union Government in India has notified the provisions of the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, aimed at regulating sports governance at the grassroots level. 

A Delhi High Court judge, Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma, has quashed a proclamation order and Look-Out Circular against an Australian citizen, stating that no effort was made to serve the accused abroad. 

A court has found Meta and YouTube negligent in a social media addiction lawsuit, according to a report by Business Standard.

Finance.summary:
The Central Authority for Advance Rulings (CAAR) in Delhi rejected an application due to a pre-existing High Court judgment on the classification of roasted arecanuts. 

The Reserve Bank of India approved the voluntary amalgamation of The Bapunagar Mahila Co-operative Bank Ltd. with Shri Vinayak Sahakari Bank Ltd. under the Banking Regulation Act. 

The Government of India stated that the country has 60 days of oil stocks and one month of LPG supplies, while Nayara Energy increased petrol and diesel prices.

India has been allowed passage through the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway, along with other countries including China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan, according to Iran's foreign minister.

Health.summary:
The Delhi High Court has ruled that bipolar disorder alone does not qualify a person under the medical disability clause without a benchmark disability. This decision was made while upholding the transfer of a Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan teacher.

The Madhya Pradesh High Court has ordered the imprisonment of government officials for contempt in a service matter, citing their failure to comply with the court's orders despite being listed 22 times.

India's health insurance sector has seen significant growth, with total health insurance premiums volume exceeding Rs 1.2 lakh crore in 2024-25, a growth rate of 9 percent.

India has maintained its retail inflation target at 4 percent for the next five years, ensuring continuity and price stability according to the Reserve Bank of India.

The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) has lowered India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.1% due to global uncertainty and rising energy prices.

The Chinese economy relies on labor exploitation.

Energy.summary:
India's carbon dioxide emissions in 2025 grew at the slowest rate in two decades, increasing by 0.5% in the second half of the year. 

Iran's foreign minister stated that India is among the countries allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, along with China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan. 

India has 60 days of oil stocks and one month of LPG supplies, according to the Government, and Nayara Energy has raised petrol and diesel prices.

Civil Liberties.summary:
The Madras High Court temporarily stayed further proceedings in a hate speech case against former Tamil Nadu Minister K Ponmudi, also dispensing with his requirement to appear before a magistrate. 

The Lok Sabha passed a bill to amend the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act 2019, providing for graded punishment based on the gravity of the offense. 

The Patna High Court set aside a direction to appoint a waitlisted candidate in a civil court recruitment, stating that candidates who fail to assert their rights within a select panel cannot later claim parity with those who were selected.

The US Supreme Court's decision to strike down tariffs imposed by Donald Trump has implications for the India-US trade deal.

The Amrit Bharat Station Scheme is transforming 13 railway stations across Delhi.

articles.title: Transfer | Bipolar Disorder Alone Doesn't Qualify Under Medical Disability Clause Without Benchmark Disability: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court, while upholding the transfer of a Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan (KVS) teacher, has held that bipolar disorder, in the absence of certification of benchmark disability, does not qualify under the medical disability clause of the applicable transfer policy. A Division Bench of Justices Anil Kshetarpal and Amit Mahajan thus dismissed a writ petition challenging the Central Administrative Tribunal's order which had declined to interfere with the petitioner's transfer from Delhi

articles.title: PM Modi to attend G7 summit in France from June 15-17, focus on global economy and Hormuz security
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to attend the G7 summit in France from June 15-17. Discussions also centered on the Middle East, with both nations agreeing to coordinate on securing the vital Strai of Hormuz.

articles.title: India retains inflation target at 4% for next five years
articles.summary: India has maintained its retail inflation target at 4 percent for the Reserve Bank of India. This decision ensures continuity and price stability amidst global economic uncertainties. The framework, set for another five years until March 2031, obligates the RBI to manage monetary policy for price control. This move influences government fiscal measures, aiming for overall economic stability.

This move influences government fiscal measures, aiming for overall economic stability.

articles.title: India retains headline inflation target at 4% following review
articles.summary: India's government has kept the retail inflation target at 4 percent. This target falls within a 2 to 6 percent band. The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee is responsible for meeting this goal. This inflation targeting framework was adopted in 2016 and last reviewed in 2021. The committee comprises central bank and government appointees.

The committee comprises central bank and government appointees.

articles.title: Centre could take charge when state environmental authorities are non-functional
articles.summary: The Union Environment Ministry has proposed a draft notification allowing a centrally-appointed committee to make interim environmental decisions when state environmental impact bodies are non-functional or their terms expire. The Centre argues this ensures continuity in decision-making as state bodies re-assemble. The draft was published on March 5. This move aims to maintain regulatory oversight during gaps in state-level environmental governance.
articles.raw_summary: A centrally-appointed committee should take over the interim decisions of state environmental impact bodies when their tenures lapse, or when they are non-functional, the Union Environment Ministry has said. In a draft notification published on March 5, the Centre argues that such an arrangement would ensure “continuity” in decision-making as state bodies re-assemble between tenures. [ ]

articles.title: Statutory Authorities Cannot Defeat Appellate Orders Through Delay: Tripura High Court
articles.summary: The Tripura High Court held that statutory authorities are duty-bound to implement binding appellate directions and cannot avoid such obligations through prolonged inaction. articles.title: Punjab & Haryana High Court Denies Bail To Persons Accused Of Human Trafficking Via 'Dunki Route'
articles.summary: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has held that persons accused of operating an organised human trafficking racket facilitating illegal migration through the “Dunki Route” are not entitled to the benefit of regular bail, considering the gravity and international ramifications of the offence. The Court observed that such offences involve systematic exploitation, cross-border illegality, and large-scale financial gain. articles.title: Habeas Plea In Child Custody Case Not Maintainable When Child Is With Natural Guardian, No Imminent Threat Is Shown: P&H High Court
articles.summary: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has held that a habeas corpus petition in a child custody dispute is not maintainable when the minor is in the custody of a natural guardian, and no imminent threat to the child is demonstrated. The Court observed that custody with a natural guardian cannot ordinarily be termed illegal to invoke writ jurisdiction, in the absence of exceptional circumstances affecting the welfare of the child. articles.title: Can a Bank Ignore Customer Instructions? Supreme Court Clarifies Liability
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that banks are liable for wrongful remittances and must strictly follow customer instructions without acting unilaterally. This decision clarifies banks' legal obligations and strengthens customer protections in financial transactions. The ruling addresses disputes where banks may have acted without proper authorization, setting a precedent for future cases involving banking errors or misconduct.

articles.title: WWD 2026: Collective Groundwater Conservation Efforts in India
articles.summary: India is the world’s largest user of groundwater, with over 21.9 million wells and tubewells extracting an estimated 247 billion cubic metres annually. This invisible resource supports most of the country’s drinking water needs and sustains the livelihoods of millions of small and marginal farmers. Yet despite its centrality to India’s water security, groundwater has long been poorly understood and weakly governed.

articles.title: What insurance numbers do not reveal
articles.summary: The article examines how India's insurance penetration and density metrics—widely used to gauge insurance coverage—only reflect premium collection and not whether households are adequately protected against income loss. It argues that rapid premium growth is often misinterpreted as progress, even when financial safeguards remain weak. The analysis suggests current measures fail to capture the true effectiveness of insurance in shielding people from economic shocks.
articles.raw_summary: Insurance penetration and density are widely used measures, but they show premium collection, not how well households are protected against loss of income; as a result, premium growth is often mistaken for progress, even when financial protection remains limited

articles.title: US takes down ‘cybercrime-as-a-service’ botnets that hijacked over 3 million IoT devices globally
articles.summary: The US Justice Department dismantled four major botnets that hijacked over 3 million IoT devices worldwide, using them to launch cyberattacks. These botnets operated as 'cybercrime-as-a-service,' enabling criminals to extort victims. The operation underscores the global scale of IoT-based cyber threats, which pose risks to critical infrastructure and data security. India may need to strengthen its cybersecurity policies and IoT regulations in response to such transnational threats.
articles.raw_summary: The US Justice Department has dismantled four major botnets responsible for significant DDoS attacks that infected millions of IoT devices globally. These botnets, operating on a 'cybercrime-as-a-service' model, were used by cybercriminals to launch attacks and extort victims for payments.

articles.title: Rupee slide may fuel imported inflation risks, say experts
articles.summary: The Indian rupee is weakening, raising concerns about rising imported inflation. Higher global oil and commodity prices, coupled with increased logistics costs due to the Gulf conflict, are being passed on to consumers. Economists warn that continued conflict and elevated prices will add to future inflationary pressures. Businesses are already increasing prices as input costs rise.

articles.title: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman moves Bill to amend IBC, speed up resolution
articles.summary: A new bill aims to speed up bankruptcy resolution in India. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman introduced amendments to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. The proposed changes focus on creditor-led, out-of-court settlements. This aims to reduce delays and improve the process for resolving insolvent companies. The bill also addresses cross-border and corporate group insolvency cases.

articles.title: Israel, Iran War: Goldman Sachs slashes India growth forecast, warns currency strain will force rate hike
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs has lowered its growth forecast for India in 2026 to 5.9 percent. The firm anticipates a 50 basis points increase in policy rates. This adjustment stems from the Indian economy facing significant currency depreciation and the impact of elevated oil prices. Inflation is also expected to rise. The current account deficit may widen.

articles.title: India's private sector growth hits 3 year low as Middle East war hits demand
articles.summary: India's private sector saw its slowest growth in three years in March. Price increases from the Middle East conflict impacted domestic demand. This data signals a shift in economic activity.

articles.title: BOT snub: Rs 22,000 crore highway projects fail to attract bids
articles.summary: Private companies are shying away from four major highway projects totaling ₹22,000 crore. Concerns over contract terms, especially dispute resolution and tight timelines, are deterring bidders. Developers cite a lack of arbitration provisions and unrealistic project durations as key risks. This situation highlights a collective rejection of India's current BOT model, raising questions about future public funding for infrastructure development.

articles.summary: The report added that foreign exchange reserves remain adequate to provide a cushion against external shocks. articles.title: DarkSword spyware puts 270 million iPhones at risk, researchers warn
articles.summary: Researchers have discovered a spyware tool called DarkSword that exploits vulnerabilities in Apple's Safari browser to target iPhones. The malware can expose user data within seconds, putting up to 270 million devices at risk if not updated. Experts warn that this poses a major cybersecurity threat, particularly for users in India where smartphone penetration is high. The incident highlights the need for stronger digital security measures and user awareness.
articles.raw_summary: A newly discovered spyware tool, DarkSword, is targeting iPhones through Safari exploits, exposing data within seconds. Experts warn that up to 270 million devices may be at risk if not updated.

The article does not mention any direct Indian government actions or policies but highlights external economic factors that could influence India's trade and inflation dynamics.

articles.title: Simple stool test may detect hidden cause of chronic diarrhoea
articles.summary: A simple stool test could soon revolutionize chronic diarrhoea diagnosis in India. Researchers found measuring bile acids in one sample accurately identifies bile acid malabsorption, a commonly missed condition. This accessible test, with high accuracy, could help millions suffering from undiagnosed BAM, offering targeted treatment and improved quality of life.
